hero_subtitle: SFTY connects MAS-specific onboarding, KYC, Travel Rule, case management and audit evidence into a unified compliance workflow tailored for regulated digital asset businesses.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_title: Compliance slows when providers stay disconnected
problem_description: MAS compliance for VASPs becomes cumbersome when KYC, KYB, Travel Rule, and screening outputs remain siloed and uncoordinated. This fragmentation creates gaps in operational control, audit readiness, and the traceability of regulatory responses across jurisdictions.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY unifies KYC, KYB, Travel Rule, and screening results into a single operational layer that structures review, routes cases, preserves evidence, and aligns workflows with MAS regulatory expectations.
workflow_title: From regulatory update to internal rules
workflow_description: SFTY monitors MAS regulatory changes, maps updates to internal policies, and routes workflows through reviews, cases, decisions, evidence, and compliance reporting.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Beyond MAS checks. One compliance layer
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects fragmented MAS compliance workflows into structured decisions, cases, evidence and reporting without replacing existing provider tools or disrupting operational control.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports VASPs managing MAS-specific workflows with provider orchestration, audit-ready evidence and controlled deployment for scalable, defensible compliance operations.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance
ask_sfty_placeholder: How can SFTY support MAS compliance workflows for VASPs
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how MAS-specific rules integrate into case management
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how MAS audit evidence is preserved and reviewed
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Describe provider orchestration for MAS onboarding and reporting
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for MAS compliance teams?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides MAS compliance teams a centralized workflow layer connecting KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule, regulatory monitoring, case management and reporting for jurisdiction-specific requirements.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY support MAS Travel Rule workflows?
faq_2_answer: SFTY supports MAS Travel Rule by routing messages, tracking statuses, handling exceptions, linking cases and preserving evidence within an integrated compliance workflow.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit-ready evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves case records, decision logs and supporting audit evidence to enable traceable and defensible compliance review under MAS regulatory standards.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing MAS compliance tools?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY integrates with existing compliance providers and tools, orchestrating their outputs into connected workflows without disrupting current MAS regulatory processes.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace MAS onboarding and screening providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY sits above onboarding and screening providers, connecting their results into structured workflows rather than replacing any MAS compliance vendors.
final_title: Structure MAS compliance operations with SFTY
final_description: Review how SFTY connects MAS-specific provider outputs, workflows, cases and audit-ready evidence into a connected compliance layer.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
footer_contact_title: For supervisory, compliance and deployment discussions.
footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
